P0100.title: Mass Air Flow (MAF) Circuit Malfunction
P0100.description: The Mass Air Flow (MAF) sensor measures the amount of air entering the engine. Code P0100 indicates a general malfunction in the MAF sensor circuit, meaning the PCM detected an unexpected voltage or signal from the sensor.

P0101.title: Mass Air Flow (MAF) Circuit Range/Performance
P0101.description: Code P0101 means the MAF sensor signal is outside the expected range for the current engine operating conditions. The PCM detected that the air flow reading does not correlate with throttle position, RPM, or other sensor inputs.

P0102.title: Mass Air Flow (MAF) Circuit Low Input
P0102.description: Code P0102 indicates the PCM has detected an abnormally low voltage signal from the MAF sensor. This typically means the sensor is reporting less air flow than expected.

P0103.title: Mass Air Flow (MAF) Circuit High Input
P0103.description: Code P0103 indicates the PCM has detected an abnormally high voltage signal from the MAF sensor. This suggests the sensor is reporting more air flow than the engine can physically consume.

P0104.title: Mass Air Flow (MAF) Circuit Intermittent
P0104.description: Code P0104 means the MAF sensor signal is cutting in and out intermittently. The PCM has detected erratic or unstable voltage readings from the MAF circuit.

P0105.title: Manifold Absolute Pressure (MAP) / Barometric Pressure Circuit Malfunction
P0105.description: The MAP sensor measures intake manifold vacuum pressure to help the PCM calculate engine load. Code P0105 indicates a general malfunction in this circuit.

P0106.title: MAP/Barometric Pressure Circuit Range/Performance
P0106.description: Code P0106 means the MAP sensor output voltage is outside the expected range for the current engine operating conditions.

P0107.title: MAP/Barometric Pressure Circuit Low Input
P0107.description: Code P0107 indicates the MAP sensor is sending an abnormally low voltage to the PCM, suggesting higher vacuum than expected or a sensor fault.

P0108.title: MAP/Barometric Pressure Circuit High Input
P0108.description: Code P0108 indicates the MAP sensor is sending an abnormally high voltage to the PCM, suggesting lower vacuum than expected.

P0109.title: MAP/Barometric Pressure Circuit Intermittent
P0109.description: Code P0109 indicates an intermittent signal from the MAP/Barometric pressure sensor, where the voltage fluctuates erratically.

P0110.title: Intake Air Temperature (IAT) Circuit Malfunction
P0110.description: The IAT sensor measures the temperature of the air entering the engine. Code P0110 indicates a general malfunction in the IAT sensor circuit.

P0111.title: Intake Air Temperature (IAT) Circuit Range/Performance
P0111.description: Code P0111 means the IAT sensor reading is outside the expected range for the current conditions.

P0112.title: Intake Air Temperature (IAT) Circuit Low Input
P0112.description: Code P0112 indicates the IAT sensor is sending an abnormally low voltage signal (indicating high temperature) to the PCM.

P0113.title: Intake Air Temperature (IAT) Circuit High Input
P0113.description: Code P0113 indicates the IAT sensor is sending an abnormally high voltage signal (indicating low temperature) to the PCM.

P0114.title: Intake Air Temperature (IAT) Circuit Intermittent
P0114.description: Code P0114 indicates the IAT sensor signal is fluctuating intermittently.

P0115.title: Engine Coolant Temperature (ECT) Circuit Malfunction
P0115.description: The ECT sensor measures coolant temperature to help the PCM adjust fuel injection and ignition timing. Code P0115 indicates a general circuit malfunction.

P0116.title: Engine Coolant Temperature (ECT) Circuit Range/Performance
P0116.description: Code P0116 means the ECT sensor reading does not match the expected warm-up curve or operating temperature range.

P0117.title: Engine Coolant Temperature (ECT) Circuit Low Input
P0117.description: Code P0117 indicates the ECT sensor voltage is lower than expected, suggesting the sensor reports excessively high temperature.

P0118.title: Engine Coolant Temperature (ECT) Circuit High Input
P0118.description: Code P0118 indicates the ECT sensor voltage is higher than expected, suggesting the sensor reports excessively low temperature.

P0119.title: Engine Coolant Temperature (ECT) Circuit Intermittent
P0119.description: Code P0119 indicates the ECT sensor signal is fluctuating intermittently, causing erratic temperature readings.

P0120.title: Throttle/Pedal Position Sensor/Switch A Circuit Malfunction
P0120.description: Code P0120 indicates a malfunction in the Throttle Position Sensor (TPS) 'A' circuit. The PCM monitors the TPS to determine throttle blade angle.

P0121.title: Throttle/Pedal Position Sensor/Switch A Circuit Range/Performance
P0121.description: Code P0121 means the TPS 'A' signal is outside the expected voltage range for the current engine conditions.

P0122.title: Throttle/Pedal Position Sensor/Switch A Circuit Low Input
P0122.description: Code P0122 indicates the TPS 'A' signal voltage is below the minimum expected threshold.

P0123.title: Throttle/Pedal Position Sensor/Switch A Circuit High Input
P0123.description: Code P0123 indicates the TPS 'A' signal voltage is above the maximum expected threshold.

P0124.title: Throttle/Pedal Position Sensor/Switch A Circuit Intermittent
P0124.description: Code P0124 indicates the TPS 'A' signal is fluctuating intermittently.

P0125.title: Insufficient Coolant Temperature for Closed Loop Fuel Control
P0125.description: Code P0125 means the engine coolant is not reaching the temperature required for the PCM to enter closed-loop fuel control within the expected time.

P0126.title: Insufficient Coolant Temperature for Stable Operation
P0126.description: Code P0126 means the engine coolant temperature is not reaching or maintaining a stable operating temperature.

P0127.title: Intake Air Temperature Too High
P0127.description: Code P0127 indicates the intake air temperature sensor is reading a temperature that is too high for normal engine operation.

P0128.title: Coolant Thermostat (Coolant Temperature Below Thermostat Regulating Temperature)
P0128.description: Code P0128 indicates the engine coolant temperature has not reached the thermostat's regulating temperature within a specified time after starting.

P0129.title: Barometric Pressure Too Low
P0129.description: Code P0129 indicates the barometric pressure reading is too low for normal engine operation, which may occur at very high altitudes or with a sensor fault.

P0130.title: O2 Sensor Circuit Malfunction (Bank 1, Sensor 1)
P0130.description: Code P0130 indicates a malfunction in the upstream oxygen sensor circuit on Bank 1. This sensor monitors exhaust gases before the catalytic converter.

P0131.title: O2 Sensor Circuit Low Voltage (Bank 1, Sensor 1)
P0131.description: Code P0131 indicates the upstream O2 sensor on Bank 1 is reading a persistently low voltage, suggesting a lean condition.

P0132.title: O2 Sensor Circuit High Voltage (Bank 1, Sensor 1)
P0132.description: Code P0132 indicates the upstream O2 sensor on Bank 1 is reading a persistently high voltage, suggesting a rich condition.

P0133.title: O2 Sensor Circuit Slow Response (Bank 1, Sensor 1)
P0133.description: Code P0133 means the upstream O2 sensor on Bank 1 is switching between rich and lean too slowly, indicating a sluggish sensor response.

P0134.title: O2 Sensor Circuit No Activity Detected (Bank 1, Sensor 1)
P0134.description: Code P0134 means the PCM has detected no switching activity from the upstream O2 sensor on Bank 1, indicating the sensor may be dead.

P0135.title: O2 Sensor Heater Circuit Malfunction (Bank 1, Sensor 1)
P0135.description: Code P0135 indicates a malfunction in the heater circuit of the upstream O2 sensor on Bank 1. The heater brings the sensor to operating temperature quickly.

P0136.title: O2 Sensor Circuit Malfunction (Bank 1, Sensor 2)
P0136.description: Code P0136 indicates a malfunction in the downstream oxygen sensor circuit on Bank 1. This sensor monitors catalytic converter efficiency.

P0137.title: O2 Sensor Circuit Low Voltage (Bank 1, Sensor 2)
P0137.description: Code P0137 indicates the downstream O2 sensor on Bank 1 is reading persistently low voltage.

P0138.title: O2 Sensor Circuit High Voltage (Bank 1, Sensor 2)
P0138.description: Code P0138 indicates the downstream O2 sensor on Bank 1 is reading persistently high voltage.

P0139.title: O2 Sensor Circuit Slow Response (Bank 1, Sensor 2)
P0139.description: Code P0139 means the downstream O2 sensor on Bank 1 is switching too slowly.

P0140.title: O2 Sensor Circuit No Activity Detected (Bank 1, Sensor 2)
P0140.description: Code P0140 means the PCM detected no activity from the downstream O2 sensor on Bank 1.

P0141.title: O2 Sensor Heater Circuit Malfunction (Bank 1, Sensor 2)
P0141.description: Code P0141 indicates a malfunction in the heater circuit of the downstream O2 sensor on Bank 1.

P0142.title: O2 Sensor Circuit Malfunction (Bank 1, Sensor 3)
P0142.description: Code P0142 indicates a problem with the third oxygen sensor on Bank 1. This sensor is used in some vehicles with a secondary catalytic converter for additional monitoring.

P0170.title: Fuel Trim Malfunction (Bank 1)

P0171.title: System Too Lean (Bank 1)
P0171.description: Code P0171 indicates the engine control module has detected that the fuel system on Bank 1 is running too lean — there is too much air or too little fuel in the air-fuel mixture. The long-term fuel trim has exceeded its adjustment range.

P0172.title: System Too Rich (Bank 1)
P0172.description: Code P0172 indicates the engine control module has detected that the fuel system on Bank 1 is running too rich — there is too much fuel or too little air in the air-fuel mixture.

P0174.title: System Too Lean (Bank 2)

P0175.title: System Too Rich (Bank 2)
